name: Villacarrillo, Castellar, and Iznatoraf Loop
name_original: Villacarrillo – vuelta por Castellar y Iznatoraf
description: This difficult 37.8-mile road cycling route explores the mountainous olive groves and historic towns of Jaén, including Castellar and Iznato
region_intro: Road cycling around Villacarrillo offers diverse terrain within the province of Jaén, Andalusia. The region is characterized by rugged mountains, deep valleys, and extensive olive groves, providing varied cycling experiences. Situated next to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, routes feature significant elevation changes and panoramic views. Cyclists can navigate quiet mountain roads and expansive agricultural landscapes. Best road cycling routes around Villacarrillo The most popular road cycling route is Villacarrillo, Castellar, and Iznatoraf Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain through rural villages and olive groves. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Torreperogil loop from Villacarrillo, a difficult 48.7 miles (78.3 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and descents with views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Cazorla – Cazorla loop from Paso de Cebra, a 40.4 miles (65.0 km) trail leading through the foothills of the natural park, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Villacarrillo is defined by rugged mountains, extensive olive groves, and deep valleys. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villacarrillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Villacarrillo?
A: Road cycling around Villacarrillo offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find challenging climbs and descents within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3927364/natural-monuments-around-villacarrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, characterized by rugged mountains and deep valleys. Outside the park, routes often traverse expansive olive groves and cereal fields, providing a different, often quieter, cycling experience with rolling hills.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes near Villacarrillo?
A: Yes, Villacarrillo is known for its challenging road cycling routes, especially those venturing into the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3927364/natural-monuments-around-villacarrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes feature significant elevation gains and descents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreperogil loop from Villacarrillo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 78.3 km path with over 1280 meters of elevation gain, offering demanding climbs and rewarding views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter dramatic mountain ranges, deep valleys, and extensive pine forests within the natural park. Routes often pass by significant water features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;. Scenic viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Cabeza de la Viña Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer spectacular panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Villacarrillo?
A: Yes, many routes around Villacarrillo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009305263/villacarrillo-castellar-and-iznatoraf-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacarrillo, Castellar, and Iznatoraf Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 60.8 km route that takes you through rural villages and olive groves. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir loop from Villacarrillo&lt;/a&gt;, a 74.7 km challenging ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Villacarrillo?
A: The best time for road biking in Villacarrillo is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for cycling, allowing you to fully enjoy the region's diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter, conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Villacarrillo?
A: While many routes in the immediate mountainous areas are quite challenging, the surrounding olive groves and cereal fields offer flatter, quieter roads that can be more suitable for families or those seeking an easier ride. Look for routes that avoid significant elevation changes and stick to less trafficked rural roads for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What is the typical road surface quality like for cycling in the area?
A: The road surface quality varies. Within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3927364/natural-monuments-around-villacarrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, you'll find quiet mountain roads, which are generally well-maintained but can have some rougher patches due to the rugged terrain. Roads through the olive groves and rural areas are typically paved, offering a smooth surface for road bikes, though some smaller country roads might be less pristine.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Villacarrillo?
A: Villacarrillo itself offers various parking options within the town, from which many routes begin. For routes venturing deeper into the natural park, you can often find parking in smaller villages or designated areas near trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for recommended starting points with available parking.

Q: Are there any permits required for cycling in the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific cycling permits are required for road cycling on public roads within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3927364/natural-monuments-around-villacarrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and stay on marked roads. If you plan to venture onto specific trails or off-road sections, it's best to confirm with local park authorities.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Villacarrillo?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the region for its stunning natural beauty and diverse landscapes. The combination of challenging mountain climbs with breathtaking views and the serene rides through endless olive groves offers a unique and rewarding experience. The quiet mountain roads and the sense of immersion in nature are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes?
A: While Villacarrillo and larger towns offer a good selection of cafes and restaurants, options can be more sparse on routes deep within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3927364/natural-monuments-around-villacarrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; or through very rural areas. It's always a good idea to plan your stops, carry sufficient water and snacks, and check for amenities in smaller villages you might pass through, especially on longer rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villacarrillo?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Villacarrillo, catering to various difficulty levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the region, from the challenging mountain passes of the natural park to the rolling hills of the olive groves.
tips:
Spanish town and municipality located in the southwestern part of the Las Villas region, in the province of Jaén.
The municipality of Villacarrillense includes the population centers of Villacarrillo, which holds the capital of the region of Las Villas, Mogón, La Caleruela, Agrupación de Mogón and Arroturas.

A small town located at the foot of the Sierra Morena mountains. The town is surrounded by olive groves typical of the area that support the production of olive oil. Like many settlements in the area, Castellar dates back to prehistoric times, with historical finds from Iberian and Roman times.
The town of Villanueva del Arzobispo is located in the Sierra de las Cuatro Villas, surrounded by olive groves and cereal fields.

Hilltop village with magical narrow streets and whitewashed buildings. The old town was once surrounded by walls and a small fort. The views over the olive groves of the surrounding landscape are spectacular. Well worth the climb.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: This tour is classified as difficult. It features significant climbs and descents, including a particularly challenging ascent from Villacarrillo to Iznatoraf, which covers 2.6 km with a substantial vertical gain of 249 meters and an average gradient of 9.7%. It requires very good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mountainous, with winding roads. While many sections are well-paved, some might be narrow or have gravel and leaf litter, especially in more remote areas. Be prepared for demanding ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass several notable spots, including the Church of the Assumption of Villacarrillo, the charming Castellar Town Square and Fountain, the Villanueva del Arzobispo Town Center, and the historic Iznatoraf Old Town.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The favorable climate with mild winters and pleasant springs and autumns makes it suitable for year-round cycling. However, summers can be extremely hot, so spring and autumn are generally ideal for more comfortable riding conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the tour?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers stunning and diverse scenery. You'll ride through vast olive groves, dramatic mountain landscapes within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, and enjoy panoramic vistas of the countryside and interior valleys from higher altitudes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: As the tour starts and ends in Villacarrillo, you can typically find parking within the town. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but public parking should be available in the town center or near the starting point.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling tour suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, due to its 'difficult' classification, significant elevation gains, and challenging climbs like the one to Iznatoraf, this tour is not recommended for families or beginner cyclists. It requires a very good level of fitness and experience with road cycling in mountainous terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits or entrance fees are required for cycling on public roads in this region. The route passes through the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, which is open for public access.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike tour primarily on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a demanding road cycling tour of this nature is not practical or recommended for the animal's safety and comfort. It's best enjoyed without pets.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will find yourself on parts of the [CIMA JA10[ Iznatoraf * Puente Ortega, [CIMA JA10[ Iznatoraf * Mogón, and smaller local roads like Sendero a la Cueva de la Lobera and Camino de la Dehesa.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route traverses the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, you might occasionally spot local wildlife such as deer, squirrels, and wild boar, especially in the more forested and remote sections.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Villacarrillo
